---
title: "KV-Cache Ring Buffer"
---

This lesson is the second standalone Circle Calculus AI contract. It is meant for the engineer who asks a narrow implementation question:

```text
Given a fixed KV-cache ring-buffer size, which slot does a token write, and is an older token still live before its next same-slot overwrite?
```

The contract is finite and exact. It is not a paging policy, deployment-safety proof, throughput claim, memory-saving claim, or retrieval-quality claim.

::: {.lesson-goal}

## Goal

Learn how a KV-cache window becomes a finite-circle address contract, then inspect one token and one retained live batch with the browser widget or Python sidecar.

:::

## The Contract

The address rule is the same finite-circle rule as S1:

```text
slot(cache_size, token) = token mod cache_size
```

A token is retained at the current read point exactly when it is not in the future and its next same-slot overwrite has not happened yet:

```text
retained(cache_size, current, token)
iff
token <= current and current < token + cache_size
```

That iff is `AIM-T0069`. The earlier theorem cards prove the supporting facts: slots are bounded, adding one full cache size returns to the same slot, slot collision is equivalent to divisibility of the token gap, positive gaps smaller than the cache size do not collide, and retained live batches map distinct tokens to distinct slots.
The stale-read guard is the converse form `AIM-T0070`: for a non-future token, not being retained is exactly the next same-slot overwrite being at or before the current token.

The generated live-window certificate makes the retained set explicit:

```text
live_start(cache_size,current) = max(0, current + 1 - cache_size)
live_length(cache_size,current) = min(cache_size, current + 1)
live_tokens = live_start .. live_start + live_length - 1
```

`AIM-T0071` proves that this interval ends exactly one past the current token. `AIM-T0072` proves that membership in `live_tokens` is equivalent to retained-window membership. `AIM-T0073` proves that the generated live window maps to duplicate-free ring-buffer slots.

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="kv_cache_ring_buffer" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0069" data-dictionary-ids="COMMON-0028 COMMON-0081"></div>

::: {.warning-box}
The widget output is an explanation and executable check. It is not proof by itself. The proof source is the Lean declaration named by the theorem card.
:::

## Python Sidecar

Use the public sidecar from the repository root:

```bash
python sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/python/benchmark_kv_cache_ring_buffer.py
python sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/python/benchmark_kv_cache_ring_buffer.py --format json
python sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/python/benchmark_kv_cache_ring_buffer.py --format markdown
```

The sidecar emits one single-token window certificate, one retained-batch certificate, and one generated live-window certificate. The default window case is:

```text
cache_size = 16
current    = 31
token      = 20
slot       = 4
current slot = 15
lag        = 11
retained   = true
next overwrite = 36
overwrite after current = true
stale by overwrite boundary = false
```

The default retained batch `(20, 24, 29, 31)` maps to slots `(4, 8, 13, 15)`, all distinct inside the live window. The generated live window is tokens `16` through `31`, length `16`, mapping to slots `0` through `15`.

## Reproducible Results

Committed fixtures:

<p>KV-cache JSON: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/results/kv_cache_ring_buffer.json">kv_cache_ring_buffer.json</a></p>

<p>KV-cache Markdown: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/results/kv_cache_ring_buffer.md">kv_cache_ring_buffer.md</a></p>

The Python tests regenerate those fixtures and compare them with the committed outputs, so the book, sidecar, and result files stay aligned.

## What To Notice

- The cache is a finite circle of slots.
- The live window is a contiguous block of token positions whose same-slot overwrites are still in the future.
- The generated live-token list is equivalent to the retention predicate, not just a Python convention.
- A non-future token is stale exactly when the next same-slot overwrite is no longer in the future.
- Pairwise-distinct retained tokens in the same live window occupy pairwise-distinct slots.
- The whole generated live window occupies duplicate-free ring-buffer slots.
- A stale token may have the same residue as a live token; residue alone is not freshness.
- These theorems certify finite address safety facts, not model quality, latency, memory savings, retrieval accuracy, paging strategy, or production correctness.

## Theorem Cards

<details class="proof-shelf">
<summary>Open theorem-card shelf for the KV-cache ring-buffer contract</summary>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0059"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0060"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0061"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0062"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0063"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0064"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0065"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0066"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0067"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0068"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0069"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0070"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0071"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0072"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0073"></div>

</details>

## Dictionary

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0028"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0081"></div>

## Source Trail

<p data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY.md">Coil Attention And Memory</a></p>

<p>Lean source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/Circle/Applications/CircleAI.lean">Circle/Applications/CircleAI.lean</a></p>

<p>Python source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/circle_math/applications/circle_ai.py">circle_math/applications/circle_ai.py</a></p>

<p>Sidecar source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/python/benchmark_kv_cache_ring_buffer.py">benchmark_kv_cache_ring_buffer.py</a></p>

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="rope_certifier.qmd">Previous: RoPE Certifier</a>
<a href="physics.qmd">Next: Physics</a>
</div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="../../widgets/ai/kv_cache_ring_buffer.js"></script>
